Ad Text:
GROUP CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

ASX listed financial services firm

No two days the same

Experienced Chief Financial Officer required

Finexia Financial Group Limited (ASX:FNX) is an independent financial services firm, specialising in private credit, funds management and advisory. Finexia was listed on the ASX in 2015 and has offices in Sydney and Surfers Paradise.

As a result of structural changes within the Group, we now seek to add Chief Financial Officer to our Sydney office located in the CBD.




What you will do

In this dynamic position, your expertise will be pivotal in enhancing the strategy and operations for our financial services group.




Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership: Partner with the CEO and Board to define and execute the Group’s financial strategy, capital management, short and long-term growth objectives.

Financial Reporting & Planning: Oversee the preparation and presentation of comprehensive financial reports, budgets, and rolling forecasts for all entities.

Strategic Analysis: Lead business modelling, risk assessment, financial due diligence and strategic initiatives.

ASX Compliance: Manage all ASX listing rule requirements, including half-year and annual reporting, continuous disclosure obligations, and regulatory filings.

Tax: Manage tax compliance and planning.

Audit: Coordinate external audits and planning.

Operational Finance: Enhance and automate financial systems and processes to drive efficiency. Oversee AP, AR & payroll functions.

Commercial Advisory: Provide high-level commercial insights on legal agreements, complex contracts, and strategic partnerships.

Capital Allocation: Drive cost-efficiency and performance metrics across the Group’s portfolio.

Investor Relations: Lead the Group’s investor relations strategy, managing communications with shareholders, conducting analyst briefings, and representing the company at any investor events.




Requirements

Qualifications: Bachelor's Degree in Finance or Accounting. CA/CPA is required.

Exceptional knowledge on AASB and IAS accounting standards.

Highly skilled tax effect understanding for consolidated group entities.

Experience: 5+ years of progressive finance experience, with a significant portion in senior leadership (CFO, VP Finance, or Head of Finance) within an ASX-listed environment.

Public Markets: Proven track record in public market reporting and corporate governance.

Technical Excellence: Exceptional financial modelling capabilities and the ability to translate complex data into actionable strategic insights.

Communication: A sophisticated communicator able to engage effectively with the Board, institutional investors, and stakeholders.

Legal Acumen: Proven ability to navigate, review, and interpret complex legal and regulatory contracts.

Mindset: An energetic, proactive, and entrepreneurial attitude with meticulous attention to detail and a "hands-on" approach to problem-solving.




Finexia is no ordinary firm. Things move fast here and we respond swiftly to new opportunities and challenges. Whilst we are somewhat flexible, this role is generally fulltime in the office.

Applicants are invited to submit a resume of no more than 4 pages. Please include a brief cover letter addressed to the CEO. This letter should showcase how you can contribute towards the success of Finexia. Applications without this cover letter will not be considered.

Applications close on 30 March 2026. Only candidates shortlisted for this role will be contacted.

Applicant introductions via recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

Senior Business Analyst - Payments - FTC

Location: Melbourne (remote)
Contract length: 12 months (definite extensions)
Salary: $130-160k + super

Role Overview

As a Business Analyst, you will play a key role in bridging the gap between business needs and technical solutions. You will focus heavily on payments-related initiatives - including card systems, compliance, fraud, and system integrations, while also supporting broader digital transformation projects across core banking, CRM and digital channels.

Key Responsibilities

Lead and support payments-related initiatives with a focus on compliance, card security, Visa/Mastercard schemes, and fraud prevention.
Analyse and document business requirements for integrations across systems such as core banking, CRM, SMS gateways, data warehouses, and digital banking platforms.
Work closely with developers and architects to deliver API integrations and support microservices architecture (e.g., MuleSoft, middleware).
Ensure solutions align with the Banking Code of Practice, regulatory standards, and internal risk policies.
Translate complex technical concepts into clear business requirements and user stories.
Support end-to-end project delivery including business case development, stakeholder engagement, UAT planning, and change management.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including Product, Risk, Operations and Technology.

Required Skills & Experience

5+ years of experience as a Technical Business Analyst in the banking or financial services industry.
Demonstrated expertise in payments systems (NPP, BPAY, international payments), with a preference for deep knowledge in card payments, card security, and fraud detection.
Strong understanding of Visa/Mastercard ecosystems and compliance frameworks.
Proven experience working on API integrations, microservices, and middleware platforms (e.g., MuleSoft).
Familiarity with core banking systems, digital banking platforms, CRM tools, and data warehouses.
Strong knowledge of payments compliance and regulatory requirements in the Australian banking environment.
Experience with Banking Code of Practice and customer fairness principles.
Ability to work in agile environments and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, evolving setting.
Excellent communication, documentation, and stakeholder management skills.

If the above seems like a good fit, please apply and we will be in touch if your profile is suitable!

ECI is seeking a motivated results-oriented, National Channel Sales Manager. A strategic “hunter” hungry for generating new greenfield opportunities within the APAC region while maintaining current relationships. You will play a pivotal role in driving and growing our channel network exponentially by strengthening partnerships, developing new channels, and crushing annual quota. This position is focused on Business Intelligence (BI), Workflow Automation, and Data Collection & Reporting technologies, selling software-based solutions to partners and resellers within the APAC region.

You will help educate the market about ECI’s unique ability to transform an organization's efficiency with unique real-time insights and reporting tools into their most complex problems. Embrace value-based selling techniques and thrive in developing positive business outcomes. Our focus is quality of interactions and strength in relationships, not quantity. Strive to bring value to your selling motion and ensuring your customers are well-positioned to obtain a rapid time to value on their investment. Possess a strong understanding of the Challenger Sale, solution selling, and MEDDPICC principles. Natural ability to accurately forecast sales activity and successfully use Sales and CRM tools to close deals. To thrive in this role, you keep up with industry trends, create massive action, and have a passion for transforming customer relationships.

If you get excited about the future of sales technology, BI, workflow process automation, Software as a Service (SaaS), and solving complex C-suite and Board-level revenue problems in a fast-paced, hyper-growth environment, then join us at ECI! We have an award-winning company culture, based on employee feedback and are extremely proud to earn this award year over year. Experience the best of both worlds; encounter the dynamic feel and energy of a start-up coupled with a long and tenured record of success among a large & expansive base of customers. Come be an integral part of our diverse organization.

Do not miss the opportunity to make your mark in developing a sales channel of great strategic significance.

Expectations/Responsibilities

Conduct meetings and product demonstrations via remote sessions and in-person.

Identify, recruit and onboard new channel partners and resellers.

Build a pipeline and close net-new business through established channels.

Actively prospect and build a pipeline of new customers.

Lead the development of key deliverables such as proposals, SoWs, contracts, presentation materials, etc.

Lead client negotiations.

Manage sales activities of channel and reseller partners to generate revenue.

Provide leadership in the transition to delivery.

Coordinate with channel partners to create and execute business plans to meet sales goals.

Evaluate channel sales performance, recommend improvements and new verticals.

Educate channel about product portfolio and complimentary services offered.

Develop process improvements to optimize channel partner management activities.

Educate accounts on solution functionality, operation, and Tier I level support.

Some travel for trade shows or in-person meetings (~30%).


Requirements

Minimum 5 years’ experience selling software-based solutions focused on data collection and BI/Analytics into channels and managing partners/resellers.

Possess a demonstrated track record of success building and managing a pipeline and closing new business/achieving quota.

Coachable attitude.

Expertise in developing integrated technology-enabled business transformation propositions with supporting business cases.

Ability to work effectively across a variety of internal and external stakeholders.

Solid consultative sales experience, possess an in-depth understanding of business operations and workflow analysis, BI, process automation and alerting, data collection and reporting, and SaaS solutions.

Manage key client stakeholders and maintain effective partnerships.

Ability to effectively manage CxO and Board level relationships.

Highly developed verbal and written communication skills, develop and deliver high-quality product and sales presentations.

Demonstrates resilience, perseverance, learning mindset, tolerance to ambiguity, and possess a winning attitude despite challenges.

Excellent written and verbal communication to conduct business with executive level prospects.

Category:
Product Management & Development (Marketing & Communications)
Other (Education & Training)
Ad Text:
Employment Type: Full-time, continuing position
Remuneration: Competitive total package
Location: Kensington, NSW (Hybrid working model – 3 days in office)

About UNSW

At UNSW, we pride ourselves on being a workplace where the best people come to do their best work.  We aspire to be Australia’s global university, improving and transforming lives through excellence in research, outstanding education and a commitment to advancing a just society.

Why your role matters

Reporting to the Director of AGSM Executive Education, the Head of Product and Program Implementation provides senior leadership over the Navigator platform, product strategy, and learner success. The immediate priority is stabilising and optimising program implementation to ensure seamless transitions from contract to launch. By leading high-performing teams, this role champions the strategic reuse of learning assets and fosters a customer-centric culture that aligns delivery with evolving market needs.

This position requires a tech-savvy strategic thinker who can drive value-add efficiencies and innovation within the constraints of legacy systems. Beyond day-to-day management of three direct reports, the role is designed to elevate the unit’s strategic capability and scalability. The ideal leader will leverage technological advances to improve productivity and ensure deep integration across sales and operations, delivering high-quality, impactful learning experiences.

About the Role

You will be required to undertake and have experience in the following areas of expertise:

Prioritise the immediate stabilisation and optimisation of program implementation to ensure seamless transitions from contract handover to successful program launch.
Provide strategic leadership across the product portfolio and learner success, mentoring functional heads to build capability and align with long-term growth objectives.
Act as the business owner for the Navigator platform, leveraging tech advances and platform partnerships to drive functionality and modernise delivery within legacy constraints.
Champion the strategic reuse of learning assets and architectures to maximize operational efficiency, scalability, and return on investment.
Establish robust governance and consistent methodologies to ensure all programs are delivered on time, within budget, and to high-quality standards.
Foster a high-performance culture focused on innovation and value-add efficiency, representing the unit externally to strengthen market credibility and industry relationships.

About You

Tertiary qualifications or equivalent professional experience in a senior leadership capacity, ideally within a large, multi-disciplinary environment.
Proven track record in program and project management, with a sharp focus on the immediate stabilization and delivery of complex corporate or institutional programs.
Advanced people leadership skills to drive cultural change, value-add efficiency, and innovative thinking while navigating the constraints of legacy systems.
Strong business acumen and technical literacy, including the ability to act as a functional owner for learning delivery platforms and support technological scalability.
Exceptional stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and negotiate across diverse internal and external functions to ensure seamless contract-to-launch transitions.
Demonstrated expertise in governance, risk management, and process improvement to ensure all initiatives are delivered on time, on budget, and to high-quality standards.

For further information on the role & responsibilities, please refer to the Position Description

How to Apply: Submit your CV & Cover Letter detailing your interest and suitability for the job (as per the skills & experience bullet points in the job description) before Sunday 5 April by 11:55pm.


Please note: This opportunity is only available to current internal UNSW staff.

 

UNSW Benefits and Culture: People are at the core of everything we do. We recognise it is the contributions of our staff who make UNSW one of the best universities in Australia and the world. Our benefits include: 

Flexible Working Options (work from home, flexible hours etc) 
Additional 3 days leave during December festive period.
Career development opportunities 
Up to 50% discount on UNSW courses
Flexible 17% Superannuation contributions, additional leave-loading payments and salary sacrifice.
Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness passport)

Job queries contact: Tai James, Talent Acquisition Consultant

E: tai.james@unsw.edu.au  (applications to the address cannot be accepted, please apply online)

UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff.

 

Pre-Employment Checks

 

Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for.

These pre-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks:-

 

National and International Criminal history checks
Entitlement to work and ID checks
Working With Children Checks
Completion of a Gender-Based Violence Prevention Declaration
Verification of relevant qualifications
Verification of relevant professional membership
Employment history and reference checks
Financial responsibility assessments/checks.
Medical Checks and Assessments

Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW.

Key Points

$200k-250k p.a.

Minimum NV1 Security Clearance Required

Canberra-based role

Important

If you’re available to start in July 2026 for an initial 12-month term (with potential 12-month extensions), we encourage you to apply. There is also an immediate opportunity to join the team now, with a confirmed role through June 2026 and the possibility of extension beyond.

Overview

Our client is seeking an experienced Technical Capability Analyst to support a major Defence acquisition project. The successful candidate will work closely with Project Sponsor staff to develop key capability documentation, ensuring alignment with the One Defence Capability System (ODCS), reviewing documentation generated by Delivery Agencies, and facilitating capability traceability across all project phases. This role is pivotal in supporting pre‑Gate 2 activities, shaping industry engagement, and delivering new capability into service.

Essential Requirements

Demonstrated experience in requirements / specification development and project management within Defence capability environments.

Experience working within CASG or equivalent Defence acquisition organisations.

Proven ability to develop and maintain ODCS artefacts.

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including experience supporting ATM development and pre‑Gate 2 industry engagement.

Desirable Experience

Background in Defence systems engineering.

Experience in capability management.

Experience in the conduct of tender evaluation.

Additional Information 

Our preferred candidate will be able to tailor their approach to a dynamic capability project and work in a multi-disciplinary team. They should be comfortable working in both an independent and collaborative manner, and capable of managing multiple activities concurrently. 

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. 

Standard employee screening will be conducted including an Identity check, Residential History check, Eligibility to work in Australia check, Security clearance check, Sanctions/consolidated list check and a National Police check among others. 

We look forward to reviewing your application.

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Lending Transformation (12-Month FTC)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

We are looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support a large, strategic lending project focused on delivering an uplift to our home loan and lending capability. You will join the Lending team within the Experience Org and be dedicated to a major transformation and migration program, working closely with product, technology, operations and risk stakeholders to design and deliver high-quality customer, broker and team member outcomes.

This is a 12-month max-term contract, with the option to extend based on project needs and performance. 

Job Description


This is a hands-on role, focused on requirements, process design, data and operating model change in a fast-paced environment.

Lead end-to-end business analysis for complex lending initiatives, from discovery through to implementation and review.
Work across residential mortgage lending, platform and system change, core banking and servicing, including end-to-end mortgage operations.
Support transformation and migration activities, including data and process migration across platforms and providers.
Partner with technology, operations, credit, risk and compliance to deliver scalable, compliant solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Business Analysis & Requirements

Lead discovery, stakeholder workshops and define clear business requirements, user stories and acceptance criteria.
Translate complex needs into actionable artefacts for engineering, operations and vendors.

Process & Journey Design

Map current and future-state processes across origination, servicing and collections.
Identify opportunities for simplification, automation and improved customer/broker experience.
Ensure alignment with credit policy, regulatory obligations and risk controls.

Transformation & Migration

Support platform and operating model changes, including system migrations.
Contribute to data mapping, migration rules, reconciliation and cutover planning.

Delivery & Implementation

Work within cross-functional teams to prioritise backlogs and support delivery.
Support UAT, defect triage, test planning and readiness activities.
Track and communicate progress, risks and key milestones.

Risk, Compliance & Credit

Ensure alignment with regulatory requirements (e.g. NCCP, ASIC) and internal policies.
Embed credit rules, decisioning criteria and controls into processes and systems.

Stakeholder Engagement

Build strong relationships across business, technology and external partners.
Communicate complex topics clearly and support governance forums with concise insights and recommendations.

Qualifications


5+ years experience as a Business Analyst in financial services, with significant exposure to home loans / residential mortgage lending.
Demonstrated experience working on large transformation and/or migration programs, ideally involving core banking or lending platforms.
Hands-on experience with mortgage operations (e.g. settlements, servicing, collections, discharges, variations).
Experience working with or alongside risk, compliance and/or credit underwriting teams in a regulated lending environment.
Background in core banking systems, loan origination / servicing platforms or similar enterprise platforms is highly regarded.
Experience working in Agile or Hybrid delivery environments with cross-functional teams.

Nice to have

Experience in transformation programs involving third-party servicers or outsourced operations.
Exposure to APRA/ASIC regulatory change programs or risk transformation in a lending context.
Relevant qualifications in business, finance, information systems or related discipline

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.
Hybrid working arrangement designed to support work-life balance, while fostering meaningful connection and collaboration.
A holistic wellbeing programs offering 24/7 support, including medical, mental health, and financial wellbeing services to enable our workforce to thrive at home and work.
Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
An additional week’s Loyalty Leave each year after reaching 3 years’ service.
Wellness initiatives with a strong focus on psychological safety.

We’re committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive community at Lendi Group. We believe that a team reflecting the world around us leads to greater innovation, stronger collaboration, and a more engaging workplace.

Our culture is guided by our 3 core values: We Are Stronger United; Act Like You Own It; and Keep Home Loans Human. Our values are part of our core DNA that helps Lendi Group to attract, engage and evolve the right talent and build best-in-class products.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing, purpose-driven company that’s transforming the homeownership journey.

Ready to contribute to Lendi Group’s next chapter? Apply now and be part of something big!

#lendigroup #LI-AMH1 #LI-hybrid
